sections.title: Movable (Synovial) Joints
points: These joints are held together by ligaments and lubricated by synovial fluid.
They allow for free movement of the limbs and neck.
They are classified based on the shape of the bones and the direction they move.

rows.valB: A joystick or ball in a cup

sections.title: Pivot and Gliding Joints
points: Pivot Joint: Allows rotation (turning). Found in the Neck between the Atlas and Axis vertebrae.
Gliding (Sliding) Joint: Allows bones to slide over each other. Found in the Wrist (carpals) and Ankle (tarsals).

sections.text: The Patella (knee cap) is a special bone that protects the Hinge joint of the knee from front impact and helps the thigh muscles pull the leg straight.

cards.fact: Small bones slide or twist past each other.
cards.mnemonic: Think of Sliding on the Ice with your Ankles!

cards.fact: The 360-degree circular movement of a ball-and-socket joint.
